Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Mar-24-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUREFOX NORTH AMERICA's Motion For Change Of Venue.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Surefox North America's motion to transfer venue to San Mateo County is denied. Likewise, defendant's request for attorneys' fees is also denied. Venue is proper in San Francisco County. FEHA actions have a special venue requirement that supersedes the general venue rules. An action for a FEHA violation may be brought "in any county in the state in which the unlawful practice is alleged to have been committed, in the county in which the records relevant to the practice are maintained...or in the county in which the aggrieved person would have worked." (Gov. Code, sec. 12965, subd.(b); see also Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 487 ["The wide choice of venue afforded plaintiffs by the FEHA venue statute effectuates enforcement of that law by permitting venue in a county which plaintiffs deem the most appropriate and convenient."].)

Venue is proper because plaintiff establishes the relevant records are maintained and administered in San Francisco. Plaintiff sent a California Labor Code section 226 request to defendant, which was sent to and received by defendant in San Francisco. (See Brandes Decl., paras. 10-13.) Plaintiff's employment agreement, wage statements, W-2s and pay stubs all reflected defendant's address in San Francisco. (Id.) Defendant argues the records are electronically stored and printed; however, this does not rebut the showing that the records are maintained and administered in San Francisco. (See Mission Imports, Inc. v. Superior Court (1982) 31 Cal.3d 921, 929 [the burden is on the moving party to establish whatever facts are needed to justify transfer].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
